title: Fpga
text:
If you ever genuinely need to peek at the deb's contents, do it in a scratch directory — never extract to `/`.

### Examining FPGAs from the host

```bash
source /opt/xilinx/xrt/setup.sh
xbmgmt examine
```

Expected:

```
Device(s) Present
|BDF             ||Shell                            ||Logic UUID                            ||Device ID         ||Device Ready*  |
|----------------||---------------------------------||--------------------------------------||------------------||---------------|
|[0000:21:00.0]  ||xilinx_u55c_gen3x16_xdma_base_3  ||97088961-FEAE-DA91-52A2-1D9DFD63CCEF  ||mgmt(inst=128)    ||Yes            |
|[0000:a1:00.0]  ||xilinx_u55c_gen3x16_xdma_base_3  ||97088961-FEAE-DA91-52A2-1D9DFD63CCEF  ||mgmt(inst=129)    ||Yes            |
```

Vivado is available on the admin instance of Coder in an FPGA Flashing template: [Coder Dev](https://coder-dev.nrp-nautilus.io/login). AMD's flashing reference: [AMD/Xilinx Flashing Guide](https://adaptivesupport.amd.com/s/article/71757?language=en_US).

## Periodic health check

The fleet currently has no Prometheus exporter for FPGA-specific health, and the device plugin's "card not advertised" failure mode is silent — you only notice when a user complains. Until a proper exporter is in place, the recommended pattern is to run [`fpga-testing/verify_fpga_fleet.sh`](https://github.com/mfsada/fpga-testing) (or its successor) periodically from an admin workstation or a cluster cron.

What it checks per host:

1. Node `Ready=True`
2. `netbox.io/site` label is set and non-DaemonSet pods on the node are all `Running`/`Completed`
3. HW visible: lspci Xilinx count, lsusb FT4232H count, `/dev/ttyUSB*` count
4. k8s allocatable: `amd.com/xilinx_u55c_gen3x16_xdma_base_3-0`, `xilinx.com/fpga_jtag`, smarter-devices ttyUSB resource kinds
5. XRT health: `xbutil --version`, `lsmod` for `xocl`/`xclmgmt`, count of cards bound to `xclmgmt`, XMC paired count
